gaps.summary: Optum IM1 supported-test evidence still depends on supplier-controlled browser surfaces and pairing-pack approval that the repo harness must not mutate unattended.
gaps.smallestSafeBridge: Keep the capability row machine-linked to the current binding hash and tuple hash, then collect only masked browser evidence from the real portal during governed operator review.
gaps.followUpAction: Promote from review-required only after Optum provides a lawful unattended supported-test tenant and explicit automation consent.

gaps.summary: TPP patient IM1 evidence remains tied to supplier-managed supported-test access and linkage prerequisites that cannot be exercised safely from the repo browser harness.
gaps.smallestSafeBridge: Use the generated ledger to pin the exact binding and tuple identity, then attach masked portal proof during manual review instead of asserting unattended capture.
gaps.followUpAction: Replace the bridge only if a repeatable non-production tenant is issued for browser-safe evidence capture.

gaps.summary: The TPP transaction row still depends on provider-side local-component install checkpoints and portal state that the repo harness can only mirror, not mutate, through the lawful twin.
gaps.smallestSafeBridge: Keep the repo-owned twin as the automated proof path, record the real provider row as review-required, and require operator confirmation that the local-component prerequisites match the current binding hash.
gaps.followUpAction: Only collapse this gap after supplier-approved unattended evidence capture exists for the actual supported-test tenant.

gaps.summary: GP Connect capability evidence still relies on direct-care onboarding and network prerequisites that cannot be treated as fully automated browser capture from this repo.
gaps.smallestSafeBridge: Keep the evidence row explicit, tie it to the onboarding-bound binding hash, and require masked operator proof for HSCN, JWT, and mutual-TLS readiness.
gaps.followUpAction: Reclassify only when a sanctioned integration candidate environment supports lawful unattended evidence capture.
